articles.title: China's Oil Output Hits Record High
articles.description: China produced a record amount of crude oil last year, at 216 million tons, with natural gas production also rising substantially, the National Energy Administration reported today. articles.title: Surging Oil Prices Push Japan’s Import Bill to an Unprecedented High
articles.description: Surging oil prices have led to a significant increase in Japan’s import bill, hitting an all-time high last month of $89.46 billion, Reuters has reported, citing government data. The June total was 25.4% higher than a year earlier, as international oil prices rose by over 50% in

articles.title: Vår Energi to acquire BlueNord in $1.3bn cash-and-stock deal
articles.description: Vår Energi has agreed to acquire rival Norwegian oil and gas company BlueNord in a cash and stock deal worth Nkr12.84bn ($1.33bn).

articles.title: U.S.-Iran Conflict Threatens Long-Term Energy Shock Across Asia

articles.title: Oil Prices Climb as U.S.-Iran Conflict Shows No Signs of Slowing
articles.description: Oil prices continued to climb in early Asian trade on Wednesday as the renewed hostilities between the United States and Iran showed no signs of slowing. articles.title: China's Next Move Could Decide Where Oil Prices Go This Year
articles.description: China’s demand for crude oil imports and the pace of its refined products exports will help shape the trend in oil prices through the end of the year, alongside the supply disruptions in the Middle East. articles.title: Fossil Fuels Still Generate 57% of the World's Electricity
articles.description: When the Energy Institute reported in its Statistical Review of World Energy that the global grid is still ruled by hydrocarbons, it may have come as a surprise to some observers. articles.title: US Crude Oil Inventories Build As Hormuz Shipping Headache Drags On
articles.description: The American Petroleum Institute (API) estimated that crude oil inventories in the United States rose by 2.603 million barrels in the week ending July 17. articles.title: Energy IPOs Are Booming Thanks to AI’s Insatiable Thirst for Power

articles.title: Norway's Oil Output Beats Forecasts as Hormuz Traffic Stays Near Crisis Lows
articles.description: Norway's crude oil production beat official forecasts in June as offshore fields returned from seasonal maintenance, delivering a stronger-than-expected increase in supply at a time when global markets remain focused on disruptions to Middle Eastern exports. articles.title: Kazakhstan Suspends Major Oil Exports as Black Sea Risks Escalate
articles.description: Following a drone attack on the Caspian Pipeline Consortium (CPC) terminal along the Black Sea Coast, Kazakhstan has newly confirmed a halt to crude transfers there. articles.title: India Pulls Back From Iraqi Oil as Hormuz Turns Too Dangerous
articles.description: India's biggest state refiners are no longer willing to gamble on the Strait of Hormuz. articles.title: U.S. Says China Has Slashed Iranian Oil Purchases by 40%
articles.description: The United States says its campaign to choke off Iran's oil revenue may finally be showing results where it matters most: China. articles.title: Why Kyrgyz Citizens Are Turning Against China's Growing Footprint
articles.description: China's expanding role in Kyrgyzstan has become increasingly hard to ignore. During the first half of 2026, Kyrgyzstan issued some 61,000 work visas, according to the Foreign Ministry's Consular Department, with about 60 percent going to Chinese citizens. articles.title: Houthi Threats Ignite New Oil Price Surge
articles.description: Houthi attacks are expanding the Middle East conflict into the Red Sea, forcing tankers to reroute and pushing Brent above $91 amid growing fears of prolonged supply disruptions. articles.title: ADNOC approves $6.2bn Umm Shaif gas cap development
articles.description: ADNOC has confirmed a Dh22.6bn ($6.2bn) final investment decision (FID) to develop the Umm Shaif Gas Cap in Abu Dhabi, UAE.

articles.title: Ansaldo returns to US market with Texas gas turbine order
articles.description: Ansaldo Energia has secured a contract to supply eight gas turbines for a Texas data centre power project, marking its return to US new-build generation.

articles.title: OMV Petrom completes Neptun Deep platform installation in Black Sea
articles.description: OMV Petrom, in partnership with S.N.G.N. ROMGAZ, has completed installation of the offshore production platform for the Neptun Deep gas project in the Black Sea.

articles.title: Halliburton wins Basra Oil contract for southern Iraq field services
articles.description: Halliburton has secured a contract from Basra Oil Company (BOC) to deliver Integrated Field Management Services (IFMS) for the development of the Bin Umar and Sindbad oil and gas fields located in southern Iraq.

articles.title: Magnolia to acquire WildFire Energy in $4.1bn Eagle Ford deal
articles.description: Magnolia Oil & Gas has agreed to acquire WildFire Energy, an oil and gas producer in East Texas’s Eagle Ford Shale, in a transaction valued at approximately $4.06bn, including outstanding debt.

articles.title: Jade Gas signs non-binding deal for Red Lake phase one funding
articles.description: Jade Gas has signed a non-binding agreement to secure an estimated $771m in funding for Phase 1 development at the Red Lake CBM project in Mongolia.

articles.title: East Coast Cluster: the world’s first CCGT+CCS is advancing
articles.description: Construction is advancing on the East Coast Cluster as the UK's flagship carbon capture power project moves from planning to 2028 delivery.

articles.title: Oil prices dip as Iran cites possibility of talks with US
articles.description: Oil prices fell on 20 July, Monday, after Iran’s foreign ministry indicated that negotiations with the US could continue, citing national interests, reported Reuters.

articles.title: PDO signs six-year EPC framework with Petrojet-ENPPI
articles.description: Petroleum Development Oman (PDO) has signed a six-year engineering, procurement and construction (EPC) framework agreement with the Petrojet-ENPPI Consortium.
